title: Literal Equations and Formulas
learningTarget: I can rearrange formulas to solve for a chosen variable.
whyItMatters: Formulas in science, sports, money, and design often have more than one variable. Rearranging a formula lets you solve for the quantity you actually need.

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.studentText: A fundraiser starts with $40 donated by a sponsor. Students then earn $6 for each ticket sold. The model is M = 6t + 40.

questions.prompt: What does 40 mean in this model?

options.text: The starting amount before tickets are sold
The final amount after all tickets are sold

questions.feedback: The +40 is the starting value, also called the y-intercept.

sections.title: Watch: Finding slope and intercepts from tables
sections.watchFor: Watch how the table reveals the rate of change even when the intercept is not listed.

contextQuestions.prompt: What two quantities do you compare to find slope from a table?
contextQuestions.feedback: A strong answer says compare the change in y to the change in x.

contextQuestions.prompt: The y-intercept happens when which value is 0?
contextQuestions.feedback: The y-intercept is the y-value when x = 0.

sections.title: Translate the Numbers
sections.studentText: Slope means 'for each 1.' In M = 6t + 40, the slope 6 means the fundraiser gains $6 for each ticket. The intercept 40 means the fundraiser starts with $40 before selling any tickets.

workedExample.prompt: A phone plan costs P = 15g + 25, where g is gigabytes of data.
steps: The slope is 15, so each extra gigabyte costs $15.
The y-intercept is 25, so the base plan costs $25 before extra data.
workedExample.commonMistake: Do not describe slope as just '15.' Say what 15 means in the situation.

questions.prompt: In P = 15g + 25, what is the slope?
In P = 15g + 25, explain what the slope means in context.
questions.feedback: A strong answer says the cost increases by $15 for each additional gigabyte.

questions.prompt: A school sells spirit shirts. The model R = 12s + 50 gives revenue R after selling s shirts. What does 12 mean?
questions.feedback: The slope is the change in revenue for each shirt.

questions.prompt: Using R = 12s + 50, what is R when s = 10?
Explain what the y-intercept means in the spirit shirt model.
questions.feedback: A strong answer says the fundraiser starts with $50 before selling shirts.

gate.completion: Student attempts the video questions, practice, and exit check.
gate.mastery: At least 80% objective accuracy and 2/3 or better on contextual explanation.
gate.remediation: Use a slope/intercept sentence frame: 'For each __, the __ changes by __.'
gate.enrichment: Graph the spirit shirt model in Desmos and identify the intercept visually.
